task_completion.description: Agent logged into the Splunk web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Web UI is visible in a web browser, indicating successful authentication.

task_completion.description: Agent uploaded the threat intelligence CSV file as a lookup table file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk 'Add new' or 'Lookup table files' page showing 'threat_intel_feed.csv' being uploaded or successfully added.

task_completion.description: Agent created a lookup definition named 'threat_intel_lookup'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk 'Lookup definitions' page showing a new definition named 'threat_intel_lookup' being created or listed.

task_completion.description: Agent executed a search query to enrich events from the 'security_logs' index using the lookup.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk Search app showing an SPL query that includes 'index=security_logs' and a 'lookup' command referencing 'threat_intel_lookup' and an IP field.

task_completion.description: Agent saved the search as a report named 'Threat_Intel_Enriched_Events'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As Report' dialog or the Splunk Reports list showing the report explicitly named 'Threat_Intel_Enriched_Events'.

integrity.description: Agent used the Splunk Web UI in a browser to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Splunk web interface in a browser, rather than using curl commands or terminal scripts to configure Splunk.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Splunk application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Splunk interface is clearly visible and functional, not just a text editor with SPL queries.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the UI by directly modifying Splunk configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing direct edits to Splunk .conf files (like savedsearches.conf or transforms.conf).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected report name is exactly 'Threat_Intel_Enriched_Events'. The target index to search is 'security_logs'. The lookup definition must be named 'threat_intel_lookup' (or contain 'threat_intel'). The CSV file uploaded should be 'threat_intel_feed.csv' (containing 'threat'). Splunk credentials are admin / SplunkAdmin1!.
